source_pr.title: plan: fix a panic bug when involved join and order by.
source_pr.body:
Consider the query:
`select * from a join (select * from t limit 1) b on a.c = b.c order by b.c`
the final plan will be Join{table(a), table(b)->Projection->Limit->Order by b.c} -> Projection.
Firstly, we calucate the b.c's index as 1. When we push "order by" past join, we should update b.c's index to avoid panic.
@shenli @coocood @zimulala @XuHuaiyu PTAL

symptoms: result = tk.MustQuery("select a.c1 from t a , (select * from t1 limit 3) b where a.c1 = b.c1 order by b.c1;") (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: result = tk.MustQuery("select a.c1 from t a , (select * from t1 limit 3) b where a.c1 = b.c1 order by b.c1;") (executor/executor_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: result = tk.MustQuery("select a.c1 from t a , (select * from t1 limit 3) b where a.c1 = b.c1 order by b.c1;") (executor/executor_test.go)
